sanskrit:
श्रुतिविप्रतिपन्ना ते यदा स्थास्यति निश्चला।
समाधावचला बुद्धिस्तदा योगमवाप्स्यसि॥
transliteration:
śruti-vipratipannā te yadā sthāsyati niścalā
samādhāv acalā buddhis tadā yogam avāpsyasi
literal_meaning: When your intelligence, no longer tossed about by Vedic promises, stands unwavering in steady absorption, then you will attain yoga.
contextual_meaning: Krishna tells Arjuna that true stability comes when the mind stops chasing external rewards—even spiritual ones—and rests in self-validating awareness.
psychological_state: Post-deconditioning equanimity; mind freed from introjected parental/religious rewards.
trigger_scenario: A high-achiever realizes that the next degree, promotion, or meditation retreat will still not quiet the inner critic, and finally sits still without agenda.
anchor_text: Let your mind rest in itself, unmoved by any promise.
